---
title: "ST313_Project_2"
author: "Tianjiao Cheng"
date: "2026-01-14"
output: pdf_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = TRUE)
```

```{r packages}
# =========================
# Install packages
# =========================

library(readxl)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(forcats)
library(ggplot2)
library(skimr)
library(naniar)
library(rsample)
library(yardstick)
library(broom)
library(pROC)
library(purrr)

set.seed(123) # We can change the number inside to produce other samples. This ensures reproducibility.
```

```{r sampling_5000}
# =========================
# 1) Create and save a sample of 5,000
# =========================
# Run this section to generate the sample from the full dataset.
# application_train <- read_excel("application_train.xlsx")
# n_sample <- 5000
# stopifnot(nrow(application_train) >= n_sample)
# set.seed(123)
# sample_idx <- sample.int(n = nrow(application_train), size = n_sample, replace = FALSE)
# application_train_5000 <- application_train[sample_idx, ]
# 
# dplyr::glimpse(application_train_5000)
# summary(application_train_5000$TARGET)
# 
# # install.packages("writexl")  # do NOT install during knitting
# library(writexl)
# write_xlsx(application_train_5000, "application_train_sample5000.xlsx")
```

```{r load_data}
# ============================
# 2) Load data and create data dictionary
# ============================
data_path <- "HomeCredit Data/application_train_sample5000.xlsx"
dict_path <- "HomeCredit Data/HomeCredit_columns_description.csv"

df_raw <- readxl::read_excel(data_path)

dict <- readr::read_csv(
  dict_path,
  locale = locale(encoding = "latin1"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
)

dplyr::glimpse(df_raw)

data_dictionary <- dict %>%
  filter(Row %in% names(df_raw)) %>%
  select(Table, Row, Description)

head(data_dictionary, 15)
```

```{r select_and_clean}
# =========================
# 3) Extract key variables
# =========================
vars_keep <- c(
  "SK_ID_CURR","TARGET",
  # loan
  "AMT_INCOME_TOTAL","AMT_CREDIT","AMT_ANNUITY","AMT_GOODS_PRICE","NAME_CONTRACT_TYPE",
  "CNT_FAM_MEMBERS","CNT_CHILDREN",
  # sensitive / demographic attributes
  "CODE_GENDER","DAYS_BIRTH","NAME_EDUCATION_TYPE","NAME_FAMILY_STATUS","NAME_HOUSING_TYPE",
  "REGION_RATING_CLIENT","REGION_RATING_CLIENT_W_CITY","REGION_POPULATION_RELATIVE",
  # stability variables
  "DAYS_EMPLOYED","DAYS_REGISTRATION","DAYS_ID_PUBLISH","FLAG_OWN_CAR","FLAG_OWN_REALTY",
  # external scores + social circle risk
  "EXT_SOURCE_1","EXT_SOURCE_2","EXT_SOURCE_3",
  "OBS_30_CNT_SOCIAL_CIRCLE","DEF_30_CNT_SOCIAL_CIRCLE",
  "OBS_60_CNT_SOCIAL_CIRCLE","DEF_60_CNT_SOCIAL_CIRCLE"
)

df <- df_raw %>%
  select(any_of(vars_keep))

# =====================
# 4) Data cleaning
# =====================
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    AGE_YEARS = (-DAYS_BIRTH) / 365.25,

    DAYS_EMPLOYED_CLEAN = ifelse(DAYS_EMPLOYED == 365243, NA, DAYS_EMPLOYED),
    EMPLOY_YEARS = (-DAYS_EMPLOYED_CLEAN) / 365.25,

    REG_YEARS = (-DAYS_REGISTRATION) / 365.25,
    ID_PUBLISH_YEARS = (-DAYS_ID_PUBLISH) / 365.25,
    
    AGE_GROUP = cut(
      AGE_YEARS,
      breaks = c(-Inf, 35, 50, Inf),
      labels = c("Under35","35to50","Over50"),
      right = FALSE
    ),

    CODE_GENDER = na_if(CODE_GENDER, "XNA"),

    FLAG_OWN_CAR = as.character(FLAG_OWN_CAR),
    FLAG_OWN_REALTY = as.character(FLAG_OWN_REALTY),

    TARGET = as.integer(TARGET),
    TARGET_F = factor(TARGET, levels = c(0,1), labels = c("NoDefault","Default"))
  )

summary(df$AGE_YEARS)
table(df$TARGET_F, useNA = "ifany")
```

```{r eda_missingness}
# =========================
# 5) Missingness & basic EDA
# =========================
miss_tbl <- df %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~mean(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "missing_rate") %>%
  arrange(desc(missing_rate))

head(miss_tbl, 15)

naniar::gg_miss_var(df) + ggtitle("Missingness by variable")

df %>%
  summarise(default_rate = mean(TARGET, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  print()

df %>%
  group_by(CODE_GENDER) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), default_rate = mean(TARGET, na.rm = TRUE), .groups="drop") %>%
  print()

df %>%
  group_by(AGE_GROUP) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), default_rate = mean(TARGET, na.rm = TRUE), .groups="drop") %>%
  print()

ggplot(df, aes(x = TARGET_F)) +
  geom_bar() +
  ggtitle("Class balance (TARGET)")

ggplot(df, aes(x = CODE_GENDER, fill = TARGET_F)) +
  geom_bar(position = "fill") +
  ylab("Proportion") +
  ggtitle("Default rate by gender (proportion)")

ggplot(df, aes(x = AGE_YEARS, fill = TARGET_F)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, position = "identity", alpha = 0.6) +
  ggtitle("Age distribution by TARGET")
```

```{r split}
# =======================
# 6) Train and test split
# =======================
split <- initial_split(df %>% filter(!is.na(TARGET_F)), prop = 0.8, strata = TARGET_F)
train <- training(split)
test  <- testing(split)

protected_lookup_test <- test %>%
  select(SK_ID_CURR, AGE_GROUP)
```

```{r preprocess_no_leakage}
# =======================================
# 7) Train-fitted pre-processing 
# =======================================
mode_val <- function(x){
  ux <- na.omit(x)
  if(length(ux) == 0) return(NA)
  names(sort(table(ux), decreasing = TRUE))[1]
}

fit_preprocess <- function(train_df, feature_names){
  dat <- train_df %>% select(all_of(feature_names))

  num_cols <- names(dat)[sapply(dat, is.numeric)]
  cat_cols <- setdiff(names(dat), num_cols)

  medians <- sapply(dat[num_cols], function(z) median(z, na.rm = TRUE))
  modes   <- sapply(dat[cat_cols], mode_val)
  levels_list <- lapply(dat[cat_cols], function(z) levels(factor(z)))

  list(
    feature_names = feature_names,
    num_cols = num_cols,
    cat_cols = cat_cols,
    medians = medians,
    modes = modes,
    levels_list = levels_list
  )
}

apply_preprocess <- function(df, prep, keep_cols = c("SK_ID_CURR","TARGET","TARGET_F")){
  dat <- df %>% select(any_of(c(keep_cols, prep$feature_names)))

  # numeric impute (train medians)
  for(nm in prep$num_cols){
    if(nm %in% names(dat)){
      dat[[nm]] <- ifelse(is.na(dat[[nm]]), prep$medians[[nm]], dat[[nm]])
    }
  }

  # categorical impute (train modes) + enforce train levels
  # unseen -> "Other"
  for(nm in prep$cat_cols){
    if(nm %in% names(dat)){
      x <- as.character(dat[[nm]])
      x[is.na(x)] <- prep$modes[[nm]]

      train_levels <- prep$levels_list[[nm]]
      x[!x %in% train_levels] <- "Other"

      dat[[nm]] <- factor(x, levels = unique(c(train_levels, "Other")))
    }
  }

  dat
}

```

```{r features_and_models}
# =====================================
# 8) Define Baseline and Extended feature sets
# =====================================
baseline_features <- c(
  "AGE_YEARS",
  "AMT_INCOME_TOTAL","AMT_CREDIT","AMT_ANNUITY","AMT_GOODS_PRICE",
  "NAME_CONTRACT_TYPE",
  "CNT_FAM_MEMBERS","CNT_CHILDREN",
  "EMPLOY_YEARS",
  "REG_YEARS","ID_PUBLISH_YEARS",
  "FLAG_OWN_CAR","FLAG_OWN_REALTY"
)

extended_features <- c(
  baseline_features,
  "CODE_GENDER","NAME_EDUCATION_TYPE","NAME_FAMILY_STATUS","NAME_HOUSING_TYPE",
  "REGION_RATING_CLIENT","REGION_RATING_CLIENT_W_CITY","REGION_POPULATION_RELATIVE",
  "EXT_SOURCE_1","EXT_SOURCE_2","EXT_SOURCE_3",
  "OBS_30_CNT_SOCIAL_CIRCLE","DEF_30_CNT_SOCIAL_CIRCLE",
  "OBS_60_CNT_SOCIAL_CIRCLE","DEF_60_CNT_SOCIAL_CIRCLE"
)

prep_base <- fit_preprocess(train, baseline_features)
prep_ext  <- fit_preprocess(train, extended_features)

train_base <- apply_preprocess(train, prep_base)
test_base  <- apply_preprocess(test,  prep_base)

train_ext <- apply_preprocess(train, prep_ext)
test_ext  <- apply_preprocess(test,  prep_ext)

# =============================
# 9) Fit logistic regression models
# =============================
m_base <- glm(
  TARGET ~ .,
  data = train_base %>% select(-TARGET_F, -SK_ID_CURR),
  family = binomial()
)

m_ext <- glm(
  TARGET ~ .,
  data = train_ext %>% select(-TARGET_F, -SK_ID_CURR),
  family = binomial()
)

summary(m_base)
summary(m_ext)
```

```{r evaluation}
# =================================
# 10) Predict and evaluate the model
# =================================
eval_model <- function(model, test_dat, threshold = 0.5){
  p <- predict(model, newdata = test_dat %>% select(all_of(setdiff(names(test_dat), c("SK_ID_CURR","TARGET","TARGET_F")))), type = "response")

  out <- test_dat %>%
    mutate(
      pred_prob = as.numeric(p),
      pred_class = factor(
        ifelse(pred_prob >= threshold, "Default", "NoDefault"),
        levels = c("NoDefault","Default")
      )
    )

  auc_val <- yardstick::roc_auc_vec(
    truth = out$TARGET_F,
    estimate = out$pred_prob,
    event_level = "second"
  )
  auc <- tibble(.metric = "roc_auc", .estimate = auc_val)

  cm <- yardstick::conf_mat(out, truth = TARGET_F, estimate = pred_class)
  metrics <- yardstick::metric_set(accuracy, sens, spec, f_meas)(
    out, truth = TARGET_F, estimate = pred_class
  )

  list(pred = out, auc = auc, cm = cm, metrics = metrics)
}

res_base <- eval_model(m_base, test_base, threshold = 0.5)
res_ext  <- eval_model(m_ext,  test_ext,  threshold = 0.5)

res_base$auc
res_ext$auc

res_base$metrics
res_ext$metrics

res_base$cm
res_ext$cm

plot_roc <- function(res, title){
  roc_obj <- pROC::roc(response = res$pred$TARGET, predictor = res$pred$pred_prob, quiet = TRUE)
  plot(roc_obj, main = title)
  abline(a = 0, b = 1, lty = 2)
}
plot_roc(res_base, "ROC - Baseline logistic regression")
plot_roc(res_ext,  "ROC - Extended logistic regression")
```

```{r fairness_by_group_simple}
# ==================================
# 11) Fairness metrics by group
# ==================================
pred_ext <- res_ext$pred %>%
  left_join(protected_lookup_test, by = "SK_ID_CURR")

# quick check
stopifnot("CODE_GENDER" %in% names(pred_ext))
stopifnot("REGION_RATING_CLIENT" %in% names(pred_ext))
stopifnot("AGE_GROUP" %in% names(pred_ext))

group_fairness <- function(pred_df, group_var, threshold = 0.5){
  if(!(group_var %in% names(pred_df))){
    stop(paste0("'", group_var, "' not found. Available columns include: ",
                paste(names(pred_df), collapse = ", "),
                ". (Often caused by left_join suffixes .x/.y.)"))
  }

  d <- pred_df %>%
    mutate(
      pred = ifelse(pred_prob >= threshold, 1, 0),  # 1 = predicted default
      grp = .data[[group_var]]
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(grp))

  g <- d %>%
    group_by(grp) %>%
    summarise(
      n = n(),
      base_rate = mean(TARGET),
      pos_pred_rate = mean(pred),
      TP = sum(pred == 1 & TARGET == 1),
      FP = sum(pred == 1 & TARGET == 0),
      TN = sum(pred == 0 & TARGET == 0),
      FN = sum(pred == 0 & TARGET == 1),
      TPR = ifelse((TP + FN) == 0, NA, TP/(TP+FN)),
      FPR = ifelse((FP + TN) == 0, NA, FP/(FP+TN)),
      FNR = ifelse((TP + FN) == 0, NA, FN/(TP+FN)),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    arrange(grp)

  gaps <- g %>%
    summarise(
      demographic_parity_gap = max(pos_pred_rate, na.rm=TRUE) - min(pos_pred_rate, na.rm=TRUE),
      equal_opportunity_gap  = max(TPR, na.rm=TRUE) - min(TPR, na.rm=TRUE),
      equalized_odds_FPR_gap = max(FPR, na.rm=TRUE) - min(FPR, na.rm=TRUE)
    )

  list(by_group = g, gaps = gaps)
}

fair_gender <- group_fairness(pred_ext, "CODE_GENDER", threshold = 0.5)
fair_age    <- group_fairness(pred_ext, "AGE_GROUP", threshold = 0.5)
fair_region <- group_fairness(pred_ext, "REGION_RATING_CLIENT", threshold = 0.5)

fair_gender$by_group; fair_gender$gaps
fair_age$by_group;    fair_age$gaps
fair_region$by_group; fair_region$gaps
```

```{r threshold_tradeoff_simple}
# ==================================
# 12) Threshold trade-offs (accuracy vs fairness gaps)
# ==================================
threshold_grid <- seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.05)

tradeoff <- lapply(threshold_grid, function(th){
  tmp <- pred_ext %>%
    mutate(pred_class = factor(ifelse(pred_prob >= th, "Default","NoDefault"),
                               levels = c("NoDefault","Default")))
  acc <- yardstick::accuracy(tmp, truth = TARGET_F, estimate = pred_class)$.estimate

  fg <- group_fairness(pred_ext, "CODE_GENDER", threshold = th)$gaps

  data.frame(
    threshold = th,
    accuracy = acc,
    demographic_parity_gap = fg$demographic_parity_gap,
    equal_opportunity_gap  = fg$equal_opportunity_gap,
    equalized_odds_FPR_gap = fg$equalized_odds_FPR_gap
  )
}) %>% bind_rows()

tradeoff

ggplot(tradeoff, aes(x = threshold, y = accuracy)) +
  geom_line() +
  ggtitle("Extended model: Accuracy vs threshold")

ggplot(tradeoff, aes(x = threshold, y = equal_opportunity_gap)) +
  geom_line() +
  ggtitle("Extended model: Equal opportunity gap (gender) vs threshold")

ggplot(tradeoff, aes(x = threshold, y = demographic_parity_gap)) +
  geom_line() +
  ggtitle("Extended model: Demographic parity gap (gender) vs threshold")
```

```{r calibration_overall}
# =========================
# 13) Calibration plot (overall)
# =========================
calibration <- pred_ext %>%
  mutate(bin = ntile(pred_prob, 10)) %>%
  group_by(bin) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_pred = mean(pred_prob),
    obs_rate  = mean(TARGET),
    n = n(),
    .groups="drop"
  )

ggplot(calibration, aes(x = mean_pred, y = obs_rate)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, lty = 2) +
  ggtitle("Calibration (deciles) - Extended model") +
  xlab("Mean predicted probability") +
  ylab("Observed default rate")
```

```{r fairness_audit_full}
# ==================================
# 14) Fairness Audit (full metrics + disparities)
# ==================================

POS_LABEL <- 1
THRESH <- 0.50

audit_df <- pred_ext %>%
  mutate(
    y_true = as.integer(TARGET),
    score  = as.numeric(pred_prob),
    y_hat_default = as.integer(score >= THRESH), # undesirable outcome (default)
    y_hat_approve = as.integer(score < THRESH)  # beneficial outcome (approval)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(y_true), !is.na(score))

audit_df <- audit_df %>%
  mutate(
    REGION_RATING_CLIENT = factor(
      REGION_RATING_CLIENT,
      levels = sort(unique(REGION_RATING_CLIENT)),
      labels = paste0("Region rating: ", sort(unique(REGION_RATING_CLIENT)))
    )
  )

conf_metrics <- function(df, thresh = 0.5, pos_label = 1){
  y_true_bin <- as.integer(df$y_true == pos_label)

  # default prediction (1 = predicted default)
  y_hat_default <- as.integer(df$score >= thresh)

  TP <- sum(y_hat_default == 1 & y_true_bin == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
  FP <- sum(y_hat_default == 1 & y_true_bin == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  TN <- sum(y_hat_default == 0 & y_true_bin == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  FN <- sum(y_hat_default == 0 & y_true_bin == 1, na.rm = TRUE)

  base_rate <- mean(y_true_bin == 1, na.rm = TRUE)

  # beneficial decision: approve if not flagged as default
  approval_rate <- mean(df$score < thresh, na.rm = TRUE)

  TPR <- ifelse(TP + FN == 0, NA, TP/(TP+FN))
  FPR <- ifelse(FP + TN == 0, NA, FP/(FP+TN))
  PPV <- ifelse(TP + FP == 0, NA, TP/(TP+FP))
  NPV <- ifelse(TN + FN == 0, NA, TN/(TN+FN))
  ACC <- mean(y_hat_default == y_true_bin, na.rm = TRUE)

  tibble(
    n = nrow(df),
    base_rate = base_rate,
    approval_rate = approval_rate,
    TP = TP, FP = FP, TN = TN, FN = FN,
    TPR = TPR, FPR = FPR, PPV = PPV, NPV = NPV,
    ACC = ACC
  )
}

add_disparities <- function(tbl, group_col){
  ref <- tbl %>% arrange(desc(n)) %>% slice(1) %>% pull(!!sym(group_col))
  ref_row <- tbl %>% filter((!!sym(group_col)) == ref)

  tbl %>%
    mutate(
      ref_group = ref,
      DI_ratio = ifelse(as.numeric(ref_row$approval_rate) == 0, NA, approval_rate / as.numeric(ref_row$approval_rate)),
      DP_diff  = approval_rate - as.numeric(ref_row$approval_rate),
      EO_diff  = TPR - as.numeric(ref_row$TPR),
      FPR_diff = FPR - as.numeric(ref_row$FPR),
      PPV_diff = PPV - as.numeric(ref_row$PPV)
    )
}

report_group_fairness <- function(df, group_var, thresh = 0.5){
  out <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[group_var]])) %>%
    group_by(.data[[group_var]]) %>%
    group_modify(~ conf_metrics(.x, thresh = thresh, pos_label = POS_LABEL)) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    rename(group = !!sym(group_var))

  out2 <- add_disparities(out, "group")
  list(by_group = out2)
}

protected_vars <- c("CODE_GENDER", "AGE_GROUP", "REGION_RATING_CLIENT")

for(g in protected_vars){
  cat("\n--- Counts + base rate by", g, "---\n")
  print(
    audit_df %>%
      filter(!is.na(.data[[g]])) %>%
      group_by(.data[[g]]) %>%
      summarise(
        n = n(),
        base_rate = mean(y_true == POS_LABEL),
        mean_score = mean(score),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      arrange(desc(n))
  )

  ggplot(audit_df %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[g]])),
         aes(x = score, fill = .data[[g]])) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 30, position = "identity", alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = THRESH, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
    labs(title = paste("Score distribution by", g),
         x = "Predicted default probability",
         y = "Count") +
    theme_minimal() -> p
  print(p)
}

cat("\nThreshold =", THRESH, "(predict Default if score >= threshold)\n")

overall <- conf_metrics(audit_df, thresh = THRESH, pos_label = POS_LABEL)
cat("\n--- Overall metrics ---\n")
print(overall)

for(g in protected_vars){
  cat("\n--- Metrics by", g, "---\n")
  rep <- report_group_fairness(audit_df, g, thresh = THRESH)
  print(rep$by_group %>% select(group, n, base_rate, approval_rate, TPR, FPR, PPV, ACC))
}

for(g in protected_vars){
  cat("\n--- 80% rule check for", g, "(DI_ratio < 0.8 flagged) ---\n")
  rep <- report_group_fairness(audit_df, g, thresh = THRESH)
  print(rep$by_group %>%
          mutate(flag_80 = DI_ratio < 0.8) %>%
          select(group, n, approval_rate, ref_group, DI_ratio, flag_80))
}

threshold_grid <- seq(0.05, 0.95, by = 0.05)

sweep_one <- function(df, group_var){
  map_dfr(threshold_grid, function(t){
    tbl <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(.data[[group_var]])) %>%
  group_by(.data[[group_var]]) %>%
  group_modify(~ conf_metrics(.x, thresh = t, pos_label = POS_LABEL)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  rename(group = !!sym(group_var)) %>%
  mutate(threshold = t)

    tbl %>%
      summarise(
        selection_gap = max(approval_rate, na.rm = TRUE) - min(approval_rate, na.rm = TRUE),
        TPR_gap = max(TPR, na.rm = TRUE) - min(TPR, na.rm = TRUE),
        FPR_gap = max(FPR, na.rm = TRUE) - min(FPR, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      mutate(threshold = t)
  }) %>%
    mutate(group_var = group_var)
}

sweep_gaps <- bind_rows(
  sweep_one(audit_df, "CODE_GENDER"),
  sweep_one(audit_df, "AGE_GROUP"),
  sweep_one(audit_df, "REGION_RATING_CLIENT")
)

ggplot(sweep_gaps, aes(x = threshold, y = selection_gap, color = group_var)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "Approval rate gap (demographic parity) vs threshold",
       x = "Threshold", y = "Max–min approval rate",
       color = "Protected attribute") +
  theme_minimal() -> p1
print(p1)

ggplot(sweep_gaps, aes(x = threshold, y = TPR_gap, color = group_var)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "Equal opportunity gap (TPR) vs threshold",
       x = "Threshold", y = "Max–min TPR",
       color = "Protected attribute") +
  theme_minimal() -> p2
print(p2)

ggplot(sweep_gaps, aes(x = threshold, y = FPR_gap, color = group_var)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "Equalized odds component gap (FPR) vs threshold",
       x = "Threshold", y = "Max–min FPR",
       color = "Protected attribute") +
  theme_minimal() -> p3
print(p3)

cal_by_group <- function(df, group_var, bins = 10){
  df %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[group_var]]), !is.na(score)) %>%
    mutate(bin = ntile(score, bins)) %>%
    group_by(.data[[group_var]], bin) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_score = mean(score),
      obs_rate = mean(y_true == POS_LABEL),
      n = n(),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    rename(group = !!sym(group_var))
}

for(g in protected_vars){
  cal_tbl <- cal_by_group(audit_df, g, bins = 10)

  ggplot(cal_tbl, aes(x = mean_score, y = obs_rate, group = group)) +
    geom_line() +
    geom_point() +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, lty = 2) +
    labs(title = paste("Calibration by", g),
         x = "Mean predicted probability (bin)",
         y = "Observed default rate (bin)") +
    theme_minimal() -> p
  print(p)
}
```

```{r reproducibility}
# =========================
# 15) Reproducibility info
# =========================
sessionInfo()
```
